- The Happiness Center employs a Happiness Coach who provides personal coaching and mentoring. Shaheen Firdous works as a Happiness Coach at UMT Level 2, Bank Building, OPA Campus. The coach provides one-on-one sessions to help students with goal-setting, motivation, and personal development. - UMT has a Happiness Center with professional clinical psychologists and counselors who can help with stress, anxiety, depression, adjustment difficulties, and emotional issues. For appointments, email cc.center@umt.edu.pk (UMT members) or app.hc@umt.edu.pk (outside community). All sessions are completely confidential and supportive.

Maintaining healthy sleep is crucial during exam season but anxiety often disrupts sleep patterns. Sleep deprivation reduces concentration memory and problem-solving ability making exams harder. During peak stress periods aim for 7-8 hours of sleep even if you feel you don't have time. This sleep improves your study effectiveness more than extra study hours without sleep. Effective strategies include establishing a bedtime routine limiting screen time before bed exercising during the day and avoiding caffeine late in the day. If exam anxiety is severely disrupting your sleep contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk or visit the healthcare clinic for guidance.

Here's what I found from UMT resources:

- Maintaining healthy sleep is crucial during exam season but anxiety often disrupts sleep patterns. Sleep deprivation reduces concentration memory and problem-solving ability making exams harder. During peak stress periods aim for 7-8 hours of sleep even if you feel you don't have time. This sleep improves your study effectiveness more than extra study hours without sleep. Effective strategies include establishing a bedtime routine limiting screen time before bed exercising during the day and avoiding caffeine late in the day. If exam anxiety is severely disrupting your sleep contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk or visit the healthcare clinic for guidance.

- Exam anxiety is common but excessive anxiety can interfere with performance and wellbeing. Physical symptoms include racing heart trembling nausea and difficulty breathing. Mental symptoms include racing thoughts difficulty concentrating and fear of failure. Mild anxiety can improve focus but severe anxiety becomes disruptive. Techniques that help include deep breathing progressive muscle relaxation mindfulness and positive self-talk. The Happiness Center provides counseling specifically for exam anxiety and can teach relaxation techniques. If your exam anxiety feels overwhelming or includes panic attacks reach out immediately at cc.center@umt.edu.pk. Professional support makes a real difference in managing anxiety.

- The Spring 2026 Final Examination period runs from June 29 to July 11 with mid-term exams ending just before. Exam stress is a normal response to academic pressure but when it becomes overwhelming it can affect your performance and wellbeing. Common stress responses during exams include difficulty concentrating anxiety sleep problems and physical tension. The Happiness Center is fully staffed during exam periods to help students manage exam stress and anxiety. Plan your study schedule early use the library resources and reach out for support at cc.center@umt.edu.pk.

Remember, the **Happiness Center** (cc.center@umt.edu.pk) provides free, confidential support anytime you need it. 💙

After completing exams many students experience emotional reactions including relief exhaustion anxiety about results or disappointment. These emotions are normal and temporary. Post-exam recovery is important for mental health. Give yourself time to rest and do enjoyable activities after exams. Reconnect with friends and family. Engage in exercise outdoor time or hobbies you enjoy. Avoid dwelling on exam performance or comparing yourself to others. The emotional impact of exams can linger even after they end. If you feel sad anxious or low after exams the Happiness Center remains available for support at cc.center@umt.edu.pk. Recovery is a natural part of the academic cycle.

- Exam anxiety is common but excessive anxiety can interfere with performance and wellbeing. Physical symptoms include racing heart trembling nausea and difficulty breathing. Mental symptoms include racing thoughts difficulty concentrating and fear of failure. Mild anxiety can improve focus but severe anxiety becomes disruptive. Techniques that help include deep breathing progressive muscle relaxation mindfulness and positive self-talk. The Happiness Center provides counseling specifically for exam anxiety and can teach relaxation techniques. If your exam anxiety feels overwhelming or includes panic attacks reach out immediately at cc.center@umt.edu.pk. Professional support makes a real difference in managing anxiety.

- If you are experiencing a mental health crisis or thoughts of self-harm crisis support is available. The Office of Safety Security and Vigilance (OSS&V) can provide immediate support for serious situations. Emergency medical services can be reached at 15 or 1122. In crisis situations immediate professional support is needed not delayed. Do not isolate yourself. Tell someone you trust what you are experiencing. Reaching out is the most important first step when facing a serious mental health emergency.

Remember, the **Happiness Center** (cc.center@umt.edu.pk) provides free, confidential support anytime you need it. 💙

- Maintaining healthy sleep is crucial during exam season but anxiety often disrupts sleep patterns. Sleep deprivation reduces concentration memory and problem-solving ability making exams harder. During peak stress periods aim for 7-8 hours of sleep even if you feel you don't have time. This sleep improves your study effectiveness more than extra study hours without sleep. Effective strategies include establishing a bedtime routine limiting screen time before bed exercising during the day and avoiding caffeine late in the day. If exam anxiety is severely disrupting your sleep contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk or visit the healthcare clinic for guidance.

- Daily self-care practices can help manage stress and improve overall wellbeing. Effective strategies include getting regular sleep of 7-8 hours each night, eating nutritious meals, exercising regularly, maintaining good hygiene, spending time with friends, engaging in hobbies, and taking study breaks. Mindfulness and relaxation techniques like deep breathing can help calm anxiety in the moment. Creating a healthy daily routine with balance between work and rest is important for mental health. These basic wellness habits provide a foundation for managing academic stress.

- Exam anxiety is common but excessive anxiety can interfere with performance and wellbeing. Physical symptoms include racing heart trembling nausea and difficulty breathing. Techniques that help include deep breathing progressive muscle relaxation mindfulness and positive self-talk. The Happiness Center provides counseling specifically for exam anxiety and can teach relaxation techniques. If your exam anxiety feels overwhelming or includes panic attacks reach out immediately at cc.center@umt.edu.pk. Professional support makes a real difference in managing anxiety.

Combining structured study time at the library with small study groups and official tutoring support creates an effective exam preparation strategy. Start preparation early rather than cramming at the last minute.

- Maintaining healthy sleep is crucial during exam season but anxiety often disrupts sleep patterns. Sleep deprivation reduces concentration memory and problem-solving ability making exams harder. During peak stress periods aim for 7-8 hours of sleep even if you feel you don't have time. This sleep improves your study effectiveness more than extra study hours without sleep. Effective strategies include establishing a bedtime routine limiting screen time before bed exercising during the day and avoiding caffeine late in the day. If exam anxiety is severely disrupting your sleep contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk or visit the healthcare clinic for guidance.

Balance study time with breaks meals sleep and physical activity. Create a realistic schedule that accounts for multiple exams and deadlines. The Happiness Center can help you develop effective time management and study strategies during this critical period. Contact cc.center@umt.edu.pk to discuss exam preparation planning.

- Daily self-care practices can help manage stress and improve overall wellbeing. Effective strategies include getting regular sleep of 7-8 hours each night, eating nutritious meals, exercising regularly, maintaining good hygiene, spending time with friends, engaging in hobbies, and taking study breaks. Mindfulness and relaxation techniques like deep breathing can help calm anxiety in the moment. Creating a healthy daily routine with balance between work and rest is important for mental health. These basic wellness habits provide a foundation for managing academic stress.

- Maintaining healthy sleep is crucial during exam season but anxiety often disrupts sleep patterns. Sleep deprivation reduces concentration memory and problem-solving ability making exams harder. During peak stress periods aim for 7-8 hours of sleep even if you feel you don't have time. This sleep improves your study effectiveness more than extra study hours without sleep. Effective strategies include establishing a bedtime routine limiting screen time before bed exercising during the day and avoiding caffeine late in the day. If exam anxiety is severely disrupting your sleep contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk or visit the healthcare clinic for guidance.

- The Spring 2026 Final Examination period runs from June 29 to July 11 with mid-term exams ending just before. Exam stress is a normal response to academic pressure but when it becomes overwhelming it can affect your performance and wellbeing. Common stress responses during exams include difficulty concentrating anxiety sleep problems and physical tension. The Happiness Center is fully staffed during exam periods to help students manage exam stress and anxiety. Plan your study schedule early use the library resources and reach out for support at cc.center@umt.edu.pk.

If you are experiencing a mental health crisis or thoughts of self-harm crisis support is available. The Office of Safety Security and Vigilance (OSS&V) can provide immediate support for serious situations. Emergency medical services can be reached at 15 or 1122. In crisis situations immediate professional support is needed not delayed. Do not isolate yourself. Tell someone you trust what you are experiencing. Reaching out is the most important first step when facing a serious mental health emergency.

- The on-campus healthcare clinic offers preventive health services including health screenings and wellness assessments. Regular check-ups can help identify health issues early and maintain overall wellbeing. The healthcare staff can provide guidance on nutrition exercise sleep and stress management. They can also provide information on managing minor health concerns and preventing common student health issues like cold and flu. Preventive care helps you stay healthy and catch problems before they become serious.

- It is normal to feel stressed during academic life but persistent stress can affect your physical and mental health. Signs of unhealthy stress include difficulty concentrating, sleep problems, irritability, physical tension, or withdrawing from activities. If you notice these signs lasting more than a few weeks seek support early. The Happiness Center and campus healthcare are designed to help you address stress before it becomes overwhelming. Reaching out for help is a sign of strength not weakness.
